title: Big mobility data reveals hyperlocal air pollution exposure disparities in the Bronx, New York

data_scope.study_area: the Bronx, New York City (single borough)
data_scope.demographics: ACS income and race/ethnicity (years referenced ~2017-2020; exact vintage not restated)
data_scope.time_window: not stated verbatim in abstract / data-availability
data_scope.availability: exposure & disparity outputs public on Zenodo (10.5281/zenodo.11044847); raw mobility restricted via Spectus

core_conclusion: Coupling daily mobility of over 500,000 anonymized users with hyperlocal PM2.5 at 100 x 100-m resolution moves exposure assessment from individual tracking to a population-representative scale in the Bronx. Personal exposure and exposure disparity show distinct spatial patterns; Hispanic-majority and low-income neighborhoods are most disproportionately exposed. Race/ethnicity predicts disparity more strongly than income, and within-group variation contributes a major share of total disparity, implying generic air-quality improvement must be paired with granular, individual-targeted mitigation.
why_it_matters: Corrects the residence-based bias of conventional exposure studies by using real trajectories; scales mobility-based exposure from small wearable cohorts to a half-million-user daily sample; reframes the equity target toward specific high-exposure individuals; releases reusable open exposure and disparity layers.
why_nature: Couples space to human well-being and equity; delivers a transferable mobility-plus-hyperlocal-pollution methodology rather than a single measurement; reports a counter-intuitive, decomposable finding (race over income, within-group dominance); strongly policy-translatable for environmental justice.
added_value_to_my_research: Transferable trajectory x hyperlocal-PM2.5 operator -> foundation-model surface for multi-source data fusion
SHAP-based per-individual exposure attribution -> explainable ML for spatial prediction
Exposure overlaid with R2SFCA care access -> combined net-vulnerability index
Disparity field feeds efficiency-equity siting -> transfer of my EV-charging optimization
Activity-space segregation x exposure -> network community detection + spatial entropy
